entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: The crop Pennisetum glaucum used for developing interpopulation hybrids

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The amount of grain produced, which is higher in interpopulation hybrids than in open-pollinated varieties

entities.name: OPEN-POLLINATED VARIETIES
entities.description: Traditional crop varieties used as a baseline for comparing grain yield

entities.name: AFRICA
entities.description: The region where interpopulation hybrids of pearl millet are economically viable

entities.name: MALE STERILITY
entities.description: The condition where a plant cannot produce functional pollen, essential for seed parent populations

entities.name: SEED PARENTS
entities.description: Populations used to produce hybrid seeds

entities.name: NCD1
entities.description: Nigerian Composite, a specific pearl millet population used as a test case

entities.name: A3 CYTOPLASMIC MALE STERILITY SYSTEM
entities.description: A system used to induce and manage male sterility in pearl millet

entities.name: RECURRENT SELECTION
entities.description: A breeding method used to develop a maintainer version of NCD1 and improve sterility maintenance

entities.name: STERILITY MAINTENANCE ABILITY
entities.description: The capacity of a population to maintain male sterility over generations

entities.name: MAINTAINER VERSION OF NCD1
entities.description: A version of the NCD1 population developed to maintain male sterility

entities.name: AGRONOMIC TRAITS
entities.description: General agricultural characteristics of the pearl millet populations

entities.name: BACKCROSSING
entities.description: A breeding method used to develop a male-sterile population from a maintainer version

entities.name: 730A0
entities.description: A commercial inbred male-sterile line used as a benchmark for stability

entities.name: INTERPOPULATION HYBRIDS
entities.description: Hybrids developed between different populations of pearl millet
